scores.metric: sandbox --disable all (masked Levenshtein); build_insns 13 vs target 16 (3 short)

sessions.lever: decomp-permuter from minimal base.c; found 0-score forms in <120s; ALL are dead-code cheats (p++/p-- no-op, dead vars/uninitialized arithmetic). Ran 2 sweeps of 19 structural variants â€” all tie at floor 3. Cheat-reviewer FAIL on p++/p-- form. Confirmed: floor-3 is the honest pure-C limit. Permuter confirms byte-matching IS possible but exclusively via dead-code constructs.
sessions.permuter_finding: 0-score forms found; all are dead-code cheats per cheat-reviewer FAIL + policy analysis

sessions.lever: cc1psx calibration (next_hypothesis #2) + sibling search (next_hypothesis #3). Reapplied candidate, reconfirmed floor 3 at current HEAD. cc1psx (original PsyQ GCC 2.7.2.SN.1 via dosemu) compiled the candidate to the SAME 13-insn folded form as decompals/gcc-2.7.2 (andi/j/move via thread_jumps). Sibling-search across text1b_b.c: no matched boolean-predicate function with a comparable double-and-test-return shape. Cheat-reviewer PASSed candidate again (independent re-review). Conclusion: both GCC 2.7.2 forks fold this C identically; the target's verbose `bnez;li 1;move 0` is NOT producible from any sanctioned pure-C form.
sessions.decision_recommendation: needs-decision â€” canonical-asm authorization. Function is a registered VBlank/timer callback (D_800F1840 = func_80078EC0 in func_80078DA0); callback-table entries are commonly hand-written asm in PsyQ-era code; the 3-insn verbose form matches a hand-coded predicate that wasn't run through the compiler.

sessions.rationale: Owner ruling: canonical-asm not authorized — Variant F (register pin + dead store) proves the target bytes ARE producible from C via a forbidden lever, which is the textbook 'pure-C path exists, hasn't been found yet' situation, not 'compiler structurally can't produce these bytes'. scan_hand_coded LOW score 0, no STRONG signals, plain C body. Cycle in blocked.

reviewer.evidence: Candidate is the natural three-return form: 0 cheat-asm, 0 rules, no dead store. Registers and framelessness fall out for free. Not byte-identical (3 short) â€” GCC folds the boolean. Clean WIP, not committed.

remaining_gap.summary: Target keeps a verbose `andi v0,v0,1; bnez v0,end; li v0,1; move v0,zero` for the second test (returns 1 if p[0]&1 else 0). GCC folds `if(p[0]&1) return 1; return 0;` -> `return p[0]&1;` (boolean simplification in jump-opt), emitting `andi; jr; nop` (3 fewer insns). p->$v1 and ret->$v0 already MATCH in the candidate. The frame is already removed (frameless leaf). So the gap is ONLY the boolean if-to-return fold.
remaining_gap.rules_required_for_full_match: HEAD matched via 2 pins + a forbidden `ret=1; ret=0` dead-store trick + 5 regfix (frame-removal subu->nop @1, delete @13, $4->$2 renames @2/4/5). The candidate retires ALL of that EXCEPT it can't reproduce target's unfolded branch â€” GCC always folds the 0/1 boolean.
remaining_gap.sweep_summary: 19 structural variants tested across 2 sweeps (2026-06-15). All sanctioned forms tie at floor 3: three-return (baseline), named-intermediate reuse, goto-3assigns, double-mask, separate-load-v, ptr-to-elem, unsigned-cmp, sub-check, ternary-cond, negation (!). Nested-if/ternary give floor 4 (worse). Precompute-both and explicit-flag give floor 11 (much worse). No sanctioned form beats floor 3.

next_hypotheses: UN-TRIED LEVER CLASS — change the return-type/shape to bypass the jump-opt boolean fold trigger entirely: (a) return the andi result directly typed as a wider int the caller narrows; (b) compute the boolean via `sltu`/`sltiu` rather than an if/return-1/return-0 shape (e.g. construct the result as `(p[0] & p[1] & 1) != 0` rephrased so the compiler emits sltu, not the fold-prone branch shape); (c) inspect what the CALLER does with the return value — if it's only ever used in a bnez/beqz, a wider-than-bool return may not break callers but may defeat the fold.

Latent policy question (NOT a per-function lever): would a 4th carve-out under legitimate-volatile-interrupt-touched.md for 'IRQ-probe-mmio-single-read' apply? Requires SOTN-master evidence + would NOT close THIS function (volatile prevents load CSE, not jump-opt fold) but may unlock sibling IRQ probes. Surface separately to owner.

cc1 RTL dump: instrument cc1 with -dr/-dR to capture RTL after each pass; identify which pass (jump.c thread_jumps / combine.c / cse.c) folds the pattern. May reveal a structure that bypasses the fold trigger.

cc1psx calibration: run original PsyQ cc1psx on the baseline form to see if it ALSO folds to 3 insns. If it doesn't fold, confirms compiler divergence; if it does, confirms the source was structurally different.
Sibling function search: find a matched sibling in text1b_b.c with a similar boolean-return pattern and study what C form was used to defeat the fold.

Accepted limitation: if no pure-C form defeats the fold, the function may need canonical-asm authorization (the 3-insn gap is a `bnez + li $v0,1 + addu $v0,$0,$0` sequence that appears to be verbatim target's branch-delay-slot fill â€” possibly the original source had dead code or an early PS1 toolchain that didn't apply this fold).

rejected_forms.reason: FORBIDDEN dead store (register-alloc-pure-c Lever D). It defeats the fold by writing a dead `ret=1` -> `li v0,1`, but that's the exact coercion the policy forbids. Do not re-derive.

rejected_forms.form: shared-end-label goto form (ret var, all paths goto end)
rejected_forms.reason: Same floor as three-return but no better â€” GCC still folds (ret is 0 in 2 of 3 paths). Three-return is cleaner.
Folds identically. No effect vs the bare three-return.

CHEAT â€” cheat-reviewer FAIL 2026-06-15. Dead pointer arithmetic round-trip with no semantic effect. Defeats fold purely by disturbing GCC's optimizer. No SOTN sanction. See rejected/ptr-noop-cheat.c.

rejected_forms.form: dead-computation forms (permuter: new_var*0, empty if, (double) cast, long long, uninitialized vars)
rejected_forms.reason: CHEAT â€” all permuter forms used dead code/UB/type confusion to defeat fold. No sanctioned form. See rejected/dead-computation-cheat.c.

prior_sessions_summary: 2026-06-14: removed 2 pins (p asm $3, volatile ret asm $2) + made 5 regfix rules unnecessary; three-return form -> p in $v1, ret in $v0, frameless, naturally | floor 10->3 | shared-end-label
